review_text: Friendly staff, clean amenities,  excellent location

This is our second stay here on our way up North. It’s a lovely park, right on the beach with friendly staff, spotlessly clean amenities and a short drive into Geraldton. Highly recommend and we will certainly be staying again next time we are in Geraldton
Caravan park was good staff excellent ( Lauren spectacular)
Beautiful park, great staff..would certainly stay again...just lived it

The location is great, right on the beach. We stayed in the villa, it was modern, super clean & had everything we needed. Stayed 4 nights. Staff were very friendly & accommodating as we had to change our original booking at short notice.
stubborn and inflexible staff

Our family of 4 enjoyed our 3 night stay in the Pet Friendly Chalet over Easter. Although the accommodation was small, it was enough and the space was clean, tidy, comfortable and we were happy we could bring our dog. The beach was only a few steps away, couldn't have asked for a better location.
Love this Park it has everything and also the Dog friendly Cabinas which is amazing, we can bring our little Ruby with us. Especially if we have medical appointments

This park is an absolute rip off, we had to cancel out stay here due to health reason and Doctors orders which did not allow us to continue our journey. I rang the park to cancel only to find they charged a $30 cancellation fee, admin costs, I know their ridiculous policy states this, but really!! the one night deposit was $53.55 and they took over half, what for to push a button to cancel and another button for the refund. One thing is for sure we will never patronise this park ever again. By the way we cancelled over 14 days out. User beware.
Fantastic service, bathrooms clean, nice quiet park.

I've just spent 4 days at Sunset Holiday Park in Geraldton.
Thank you Anna and stsff.
We had a 'van unit' which was great.
Clean and well presented.

Lovely break, thanks again

Gaye and Ross

We only stayed 1 night as a stop over. The staff was lovely, bathrooms are very clean, site was a decent size. There is a nice play area for kids to burn of some energy. The treat was that you walk out directly onto the beach. IGA and servo is just down the street if you need to restock so the location is perfect. Would recommend this park anytime.
I visited here with my wife for our trip to Broome. The accommodation was very clean and easy to navigate around. Thanks Sarah for the cleaning the room making it hygienic!
Lovely location, quiet beach, dog friendly and very well maintained.  Handy for town and resupplying.  Can't really fault it,
Loved this place spacious sites close to beach and very quiet. We only did a stopover this time but great place.
Really appreciated being able to book an air-conditioned, pet friendly cabin for our elderly dog. The cabin was very clean, comfy bed, right on the beach, friendly staff and locals.

Great spot, right on the beach. Good, clean amenities, but they could do with a bit of love. Lovely little stall outside of reception selling tomatoes & a coffee van on Thursdays. They have a sunset viewing platform, but the shrubs are getting too high! Sunset on the beach is awesome 👌

great caravan park in Geraldton, always stay here when passing through Geraldton.|close to everything walking distance to local IGA and fish and chip shop. sites good size  clean toilet and showers. very close to beach
Beautiful place close to the sea, friendly staff, clean shower and toilet, clean kitchen and common area. I highly recommend this campsite
Very welcoming, friendly nice people,hope to get back soon,very professional and a clean park👍😄

I stay a couple night as a stop off heading north from Perth and on return.
Great site to stay as close to shops and right beside the beach. Gounds and buildings well maintained.
Will return again
Beautiful spot, very clean facilities and friendly staff. We had a great time with the kids and our dog!!

Always have agood stay.
Kerp up the good work

Great location, close to the town centre with an IGA just around the corner. We stayed in an ocean-view cabin! The cabin itself was a bit dated and quite basic inside, but it was clean and the location made up for it. The deck was lovely to chill out on.

There’s direct beach access from within the park to a beautiful beach and a short walk down you’ll even find pink sand.

The pool was great, and the camp kitchen next door was excellent… very spacious with an oven, fridge, microwave, TV and plenty of bench space.

The kids’ playground was also really good, with equipment suitable for different age groups. Next to the playground are BBQs and a gazebo, plus a shaded tree nearby where parents can sit, though some additional shade over the play equipment would be a great improvement ☺️

3 adults, we stayed over Xmas. Our cottage was spotlessly clean and nicely decorated. It was great to go to sleep hearing the waves break behind the cottage. |On the negative side -  we had 3 bath towels but no hand towels, face washers , tissues or shower caps. There was no-one to ask as the office was closed for Xmas day. I think this could be easily rectified. Otherwise good.
The place was good and safe. I liked the atmosphere of camping site or caravan park

Never come back again. I booked with 2 bed room chalet but they gave us 2 bed room cabin with wheelchair access for some reason. It’s totally different room as picture showed. The cabin was very old and cleaning was not completed. Dogs hair was on the floor in the bathroom which was really disappointing. I wonder if they organized cleaning before we arrived.
We stayed over the Christmas. We expected check in before 2pm but they made us rush to check in. It’s not worth it at all.

Update 2025: schattige Plätze gibt's nur wenige, guter Service, man kann auch nach den Öffnungszeiten über eine Notfall Nummer einchecken. Schöner gepflegter Pool und endloser Strand. Es gibt einen Rabatt für Surfer 😊
Update 2023: Leider alle Bäume gefällt Schatten Mangelware und die Preise kräftig erhöht
Perfect place to stay
캠핑장 옆에 바로 beach가 있으며 조용하고 깨끗했다.

Nice location but lawns are dead, had no hot water on arrival,  can't get WiFi in cabin so tv doesn't work and we had tell tell the builders building the cabin next to us to be quite at 630 on a Saturday, bed was uncomfortable and on wheel so had to jam the dresser to stop it rollering around the room, once we did get hot water the shower was like needles, wouldn't recommend.

Spiders and cockroaches in the room. The floor would cave in in some places you stepped...kept clear of those areas.
Lovely staff at the front counter and gorgeous views though.
Great caravan park right on the beach
A nice little accommodation right on the beach

Lovely spot.. always worth asking for a spot which is a bit protected from the wind.
Facilities are great, nice pool and lovely sunset. you can watch and have a wine on the special platform they have constructed right on beach..

Great place to stay right on the beach ⛱️ little windy but bearable.
Staff are awesome helped us out with a small problem on our MH  good clean facilities, walking distance to supermarket.
An absolutely beautiful caravan 🚐 park. I can't complain about anything!!
Really clean and friendly.. close to supermarket plus places to eat.
Bed was a little small for my hubby who is 6ft so his feet were hanging over lol, service was friendly and location was nice.
Good amenities. Great location for sunsets - there is even a viewing platform!

I have not rated the service as apart from checking in/out we were entirely self catering. The two bedroom chalet was like new, clean and comfortable. I rated the location as 5 star as it was very close to the family that we were visiting.
Picturesque village and views very much appreciated for the peace 🕊️
Is was great

Annie and Mike at Sunset Beach Holiday Park are fabulous.
The cabin we stayed in number 20 was amazing. Views of the ocean. The ability to be able to entertain on the balcony from the kitchen with window opening on to a bar style bench. The kitchen was fully stocked with everything needed to cook and entertain. The shower was quite large and a confy double bed. There is also a second room for kids with double bunks.
Well our vehicle battery and alternator died morning we were to check out which ment we were going nowhere fast, except to the mechanic.
Annie and Mike were gracious enough to let us stay in the same cabin another night and offered their vehicle to us if we needed to pop to the shop for groceries.
We can not praise Sunset Beach Holiday Park enough and would like to say Thank you for your graciousness and hospitality.
Nigel and Sue Levey

Right on the seaside. Caravan sites with concrete slabs. Only criticism is electrical and plumbing points are not always correctly positioned for the standard caravan settings. You need longer electrical, water and sullage pipes for this type of camp. Plus on the higher side of fees.

We rang Loreen as we were about 35 minutes away and it was right on 4.30. She confirmed we were registered as staying there previously and as we had a gift voucher she said she would wait for us to book us in. We arrived a couple of minutes after 5 and true to her word she was in the office ready to book us in. We feel she went out of her way to assist us after driving down from Shark Bay. The toilets and showers might be a bit old but they were immaculately clean and well maintained. Loreen was able to book us into a drive through site so I didn't have to unhook from car. Grounds were very well kept and sunset viewing deck was very popular. We done guys. See you on our next trip north. Lyn and Kev (Site 1)
A nice caravan park close to the beach with clean ablution blocks and friendly staff
Great park outside of Perth, crime apparently bad but the facilities here were so nice

2nd time staying here and it's one of the best we have stayed in and we get around a bit.
The management were extremely helpful when our vehicle broke down,  even offering us their vehicle to go to the shops.
The villa was very clean, spacious and had everything we needed, my only complaint was it was cloudy so no sunset ;)
We will be back :)

Our site was large enough for our car van and awning just. The amenities are a little old but kept very clean. The park has direct access to the beach 🏖️ and they also have a Sunset viewing deck which was packed the night we went so we went onto the beach. The beach is nothing special and has a sharp angle to come and with when walking. The off leash beach section is very close to the park which is great. The IGA is within walking distance and has most everything you need.
Last note, could you please clean the bathrooms after 10am to allow leaving guests use the facilities before setting off.

$249 a Night — and This Is the Welcome I Get

I’ve just arrived at Sunset Beach Holiday Park today after hours on the road. My arthritis is flaring badly — both knees aching, every step slow and heavy. I limp my way from the car to reception. Anyone watching could see the effort it’s taking.

I walk in and say, “Hi, I’ve got a booking.” I explain it might be under my name or my daughter’s. No hello back, no welcome! She checks, says it’s under my name, and immediately asks for my car registration. I tell her it should already be on file because you can’t book with booking.com without entering it. She just says she doesn’t have it. No offer to check. No attempt to make it easier for me. So I hobble back out to my car, get the number, and come back in.

When I give it to her, she asks for my bank card and driver’s licence. This stops me. I’ve stayed in plenty of places and never been asked for a driver’s licence just to check in. I ask why. She says, “It’s our policy.” That’s it. So, still sore, I go back to my car a second time to get my wallet.

By the time I return, she’s walked away from the desk. I stand there waiting, my legs throbbing. When she comes back, I hand over my details and tell her, “By the way, I do Google Reviews, I’ve had nearly 16 million views on my account. Just letting her know.” I say it so she understands: customer service matters.

Travellers in caravans, RVs, and vans often drive for hours before they stop. They want a warm welcome, a smile, and to feel glad they’ve arrived. That first interaction sets the tone for the whole stay. Today, mine was cold, unhelpful, and made harder than it needed to be. If I was her manager, I would deal with her manner directly and explain that customers can make or break a place like this. You have to greet guests warmly and treat them well, especially after they’ve been on the road for hours.

And it’s a shame, because the place itself is lovely. My cabin is clean and stocked with the usual shampoo and body wash in the bathroom. There’s a microwave, air fryer, jug, toaster, and fridge,  everything you need except the food. The camp facilities are also good, with clean, well-kept kitchens, an easy-access dump point for caravans, and neat ablution blocks. The park is just steps from a beautiful beach, with picnic tables and grassed areas to enjoy the ocean view. It’s got everything you need to have a great stay, if the welcome matched the setting.

The unit walls are paper thin and you can hear your neighbours doing their business clearly. Not the type of things you need or want to hear

But the first impression is already soured. I arrived sore and tired, hoping for a welcome. Instead, I was met with policies and indifference. Facilities can be wonderful, but if the greeting falls flat, the whole experience is tainted from the start.

Great park, staff are friendly, helpfull.
The dog friendly cabin is a ripper and rclose to the beach.

Booked a powered site for 1 night. Reception was very helpful with after hours check-in. Arrived at 8.30pm and could easily access my site however there was smashed glass on the grass which was difficult to navigate in the dark.

Due to my mistake, I accidentally made a reservation for the same date next year, but the staff kindly came to help me even though it was outside reception hours. They corrected the booking, and we were able to stay without any problems. Since this was the first day of our road trip, I was relieved that our journey wasn’t ruined. The access to the beach was great, and the sunset from there was truly breathtaking.
Room was great. What surprises me was that no hair dryer was provided. For that, I will have to deduct one star for the overrall rating

Great location.

Our Ranger broke down, and after getting messed around by most of the towing companies and several so called diesel workshops, we finally got towed in here. Was such a nice place to base ourselves while our vehicle was repaired.

Freindly staff, great locaiton.
Good location and facilities.
Great location, nice clean en suite slabbed site. Staff were nice as well. There was a BBQ type van on site that sold absolutely lush beef brisket burgers.
This place is very clean and a great location. The camp kitchen is very good and it even has a pool table which is a bonus
The cabins are well appointed but pretty small.  The ones with cooking facilities do not have extractor fans.  Camping and caravan facilities are very close together.

Annie and the team were beyond amazing. we were super stuck and Annie went above and beyond for us. the most accommodating, lovely person we have met in any campsite across Australia.

the campsite itself is wonderful. stones throw from a surfing beach, easy to get into town. highly recommend. The cabins are lovely

This is a great park to stay whilst visiting Geraldton (or Gerro to the locals).  About 10 min drive north of the city and away from any traffic noise.  Site was a coctrete slab for the annex.  Facilities are old but clean.  Pool is refreshing on a hot day.  Great camp kitchen and sandy playground for the kids.
The elevated platform overlooking the beach is a great place for a drink and watch the amazing sunsets
We had a caravan repair booked in which took shorter than was thought and the team here were nothing short of fabulous in changing us a couple of times between a site and their cabins!
Definitely recommended!
Site  small for $60 overnight for caravan no space for awning

Enjoyed our peaceful and relaxing two night stay in a  new  2 x 2 chalet. It was very clean and everything we needed was provided, except there were no towel rails and a tiny milk for 2 couples was not enough.  Just down the road is a IGA, chemist, news agency and a bottle shop but it would have been great to have a bakery on site.|The smell of freshly baked bread in the morning would have been amazing to wake up to and with the sound of the waves in the background.|The best location we have stayed in Geraldton.|Check in and check out was very easy and we would stay again.
Amazing place
Lovely quiet park, tucked away behind the dunes, quick access for fishing and gorgeous sunset shots with a wine
Lovely park friendly service held a site for us witch we were very thankful for the sunset here are phenomenal clean park nice amenities we will definitely be back.
Nice caravan park on the beach.  Only 1 night but it was a nice park to rest up.
I asked for a drive thru, yahoo, got one, however, you can't use your awning as they are too narrow, WT

Courteous receptionist & groundsman. Good sized caravan sites. Great camp kitchen with appliances and table settings. Swimming pool and playground. Dry weather of late so dusty. Lawn areas are being watered.
A viewing platform to watch the sunset.
Very friendly, helpful and caring staff.
Nice park clean tidy and quite great location on the beach front.
Stayed in the smaller dong like cabins which for a queen bed and twin share had one cup, one plate! However there is exclusive access to a camp kitchen which was awesome!
I lost one bicycle and some food!
We have always stayed there when every we go to Gero lovely place
Only stayed the one night on way to coral bay but nice big camp pitch and good amenities. Overall nice clean camp-site, will have to get there again and stay for longer

good camping ground for a night or two. big spaces, beach with great sunset to walk to. late checkin worked without any hassle. toilets&showers aswell as the bin situation was at times not so clean, otherwise all was okay👍🏼

Stayed a night on our trip to the north. You have to call for a tent spot and reservation and payment through the phone went well. They gave us a spot in the full sun without any grass, which was not ideal for pitching a tent (and 40+ degrees..) But we asked for another spot and could relocate: easy! The nice tent spots (with shade) are 84 until 89, so I would request one of those. Great access to the beach, clean facilities and big communal kitchen with stoves, fridges etc.

Très bel endroit, venteux 😀.
Accueil excellent, équipements impeccables.
Allez-y !

They have ruined our trip, we couldn’t sleep in a small bed which they have advertised as double bed. Lots of ants and spiders were crawling on the floor. We couldn’t sleep well in their bed which costed us 195$/ night. So sad we have been scammed

I walked in on a weekday expecting at least a decent place to sleep for a few nights—for a few hundred dollars a night, that shouldn’t be too much to ask, right? Wrong. What I got instead was an absolute joke of a room that looked NOTHING like the dodgy, deceptive photos they use to trick people into booking.||The room itself was tiny, barely enough space to move without bumping into furniture. It felt more like a cramped storage closet than a hotel room. And the worst part? It was absolutely filthy. I found spiders in the corners, ants crawling across the floor, and dust piling up like the place hadn’t been cleaned in weeks. The level of neglect was disgusting.||The bathroom was a nightmare. The moment I stepped in, I was hit with a strong, musty stench—a mix of dampness, mildew, and something else I don’t even want to think about. The sink and shower were covered in rust stains, and honestly, I didn’t even feel comfortable using them. At this price point, the least they could do is provide a bathroom that doesn’t look and smell like it’s been left to rot.||And then there was the bed. What a joke. I literally didn’t fit—my ankles stuck out over the edge, and the mattress was so uncomfortable I might as well have slept on the floor. I barely got any rest, constantly tossing and turning, wondering how a place charging this much could get away with offering such a miserable experience.||For this price, I expected comfort and cleanliness. Instead, I got a claustrophobic, dirty, bug-infested dump. Save yourself the frustration and book literally anywhere else.

A Complete Waste of Money – Do Yourself a Favor and Stay Away!

I walked in on a weekday expecting at least a decent place to sleep for a few nights—for a few hundred dollars a night, that shouldn’t be too much to ask, right? Wrong. What I got instead was an absolute joke of a room that looked NOTHING like the dodgy, deceptive photos they use to trick people into booking.

The bed? An absolute disaster. I literally didn’t fit—my ankles were hanging off the edge all night like I was sleeping in a child’s bed. How do you charge this much and not even provide a bed fit for an adult?! The mattress was so uncomfortable I might as well have slept on the floor. Is it too much to ask that, after paying hundreds of dollars, I actually get a good night’s sleep?!

And let’s talk about the amenities—or should I say, the absolute lack of them. The whole place felt neglected, like no one actually cares about guests once they’ve taken your money. I felt scammed, plain and simple.

Bottom line: DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. For this price, you deserve to sleep comfortably and get what you paid for. Instead, you’ll leave tired, frustrated, and wondering why you didn’t just book somewhere else. I know I won’t make this mistake again.
Best park in Geraldton by far. Right on the beach and easy access to IGA and shops 2 blocks away. Great camp kitchens and spotless amenities.
Overpriced

This is an amazing holiday park!! Only about 4hrs from Perth.
The only one right on the beach in Geraldton.
Staff are friendly and helpful.
Clean facilities, pets welcome, bbqs near playground.
Watched the sun set from the seated lookout with a bottle of our favourite wine.
Shopping center within an easy 15min walk or minute drive at the end of the street.
Love this place, great for a quick get away!!!

I stayed here for an extended period. It underwent new management last year who are very friendly always keen to help with anything. I found the staff at reception and around the park were also polite and diligent. The camp kitchen and ablution blocks were cleaned thoroughly each day. The park is in a beautiful location and it's name says it all with magnificent sunsets each evening. I will happily stay again next summer.
Lost 1 star due to renovation noise at 7am. Could have placed us further away as quite a few empty cabins available

We had the pleasure of staying here over the Australia Day Weekend after our originally booked accommodation was disgusting on arrival. Luckily for us the beautiful owners had a cancellation and booked us into one of their chalets. Accommodation was perfect! Beautifully clean, perfect location. Customer service was outstanding. Thank you so much for saving my birthday weekend! We will most definitely be back!
Great friendly hoists very quiet park with good facilities
바로 앞에 일몰감상 및 서핑 가능한 바다가 있어 위치좋아요! 캠핑사이트랑 숙소가 같이 있어서 일반 투숙객도 이용 가능합니다. BIG4 그룹에서는 이제 빠졌고, 시설은 좀 낡았습니다.

Auch ein Park zu dem wir immer wieder fahren würden. Alles war super. Personal ist sehr hilfsbereit und freundlich. Direkt am Ozean gelegen. Wunderschöne Sonnenuntergänge. Toll fanden wir den Kleinverkauf am Eingang von günstigem, örtlichem Gemüse. Es gibt nichts zu kritisieren, nur zu loben. Auch die Umgebung bietet alles, was man so braucht und sehen möchte.
I needed an 8 to 10 week stay to get out of a DV situation.  I am a local of 8 years with my own caravan.  They could only offer me 14 days.
Camped there, no shade, very basic and expensive for what it was. Pool ok but very small, amenities ok but dated. Beach was poor.
Nice beach

화장실, 샤워장 낡았지만 사용하기 괜찮았어요.
퇴실할때 대여한 캠핑카에 문제가 생겨서 체크인하우스 앞에 주차를 하고 고칠만한 도구를 빌려줄 수 있느냐고 요청했는데 없다고 하더군요. 관리직원을 보내준다고 해서 밖에서 한참을 기다렸는데 안와서 다시 들어가 사진을 보여주며 몽키스패너 만 빌려줄 수 없느냐 하니 다시 없다고 하고는 그 캠핑장에서 묵은게 맞냐고 물어보더군요. 응대가 매우 기분이 나빴습니다.

We had a great stay at the park in one of the cabins. It was clean and comfortable. The new management were incredibly helpful, proactive and friendly. The beach is beautiful. Supermarket just up the road. We woke to the sound of the ocean and pretty sounding birds, not crows! Ripper! Would recommend.

New management are out to improve the setting with painting of facilities and ablutions upgrades.|At least 2 camp kitchens and ablutions areas easily accessible from our caravan site.|Crystal clear pool, spacious camp kitchens.|Beautiful beach and sunsets from the viewing deck.|

We moved from a noisey camp to this glorious caravan park!  I’m not a fan of parks but this one was amazing!
Facilities were immaculate (and I’m a facilities coordinator) so I was impressed!
The camp kitchens were modern and had everything you needed, and one was massive!
BBQs outside also, lovely pool, sunset deck over the beach and lots of bins, great laundry also. What more could you want?!!
Our site was large, staff friendly and we found it quiet and peaceful.
Wish we had booked here from the beginning of our stay in Geraldton!
Very Peaceful and 10 minutes from CBD !!!!!
Have stayed several times before due to location.  Caravan sites seem to be run down  . Ablutions  clean but need upgrade .

In short: a nice, basic and clean experience at 50m from the ocean.
I was here a week for work and the cabin had all it needed.
Advertised for up to two people.
I was alone and it was perfect, for two people it would have been too small.
Having said that: if you are only crashing here for the one night you should be OK.

Great facilities and during a walk around there was ample space where all the grey nomads we parked with their caravans etc...

One thing stopping giving it a 5 is the ever lacking logistics of doing the washing.

No money change, reception does not change money, I asked if they could charge money to our current account, they couldnt.
I was send to a local shop that charged me $4.50 for changing money.

Too much hassle guys.

why can't you equip the machines with eftpos, way easier, we are heading to a cashless society but that era has not arrived here.
Possible fees you incorparate in the price.

Despite this it's lovely holiday 50meters from the ocean.

We stayed at this park earlier in the week on a powered site and the second night we were there our Engel freezer was stolen from the back of our vehicle by cockroaches who prowl around in the dark and steal things that do not belong to them. We were not warned by the Park staff when we checked in that theft and security was an issue or we would not have stayed there and we will definitely not be staying again.

I will preface by saying, I have travelled a lot in my time, including camping and backpacking in third world countries and am quite happy to rough it. The cabin we were allocated was quite possibly one of the filthiest places I’ve ever seen. There were black hairs on the linen, unidentified smears of brown and green substances on the furniture, rotting wood underneath the kitchen area, mould in the bathroom, a layer of thick dust along the window panes, the list goes on. The rate for the night was over $180.  I spent 5 minutes inside and left. Thankfully, we received a full refund and were lucky to find another option. Sharing our feedback because arriving here unknowingly with limited accomodation options could leave you stranded in Geraldton.
We stayed in the Caravan park. The amenities and laundry facilities were clean. Great playground for the kids.

Stayed in a powered caravan site for 4 nights in July 2024 while in Geraldton for a sporting event. Park is located a little bit North of town, right on the beach. We had a large grassed site with plenty of room. Amenities are dated but clean. Walk path through to river mouth and grassy park. Raised platform to watch the sunsets. Unfortunately, our neighbours had their Engel fridge stolen overnight but the park management does advise you to keep your valuables locked up.

Everything worked. Site was fine. It’s an old park and I guess only just taken up by Big4. Still a lot of investment needed.
No one expects a cuddle on arrival, however at least a smile and an acknowledgment would have been nice.
Stopped through for 1 night, great access for after hours. Super efficient and easy. Facilities average.

Our first time in a cabin at a caravan park and it has set the standards. My family loved it here, rooms and toilets are clean, fresh towels and basic toiletries were provided. Situated right next to the ocean was perfect for a morning stroll.

Great access to the beach
Viewing platform to see the big boats parked up.
Quite park .
Receptionist  needs to learn customer service skills sites can be hard to get in to

Paula, Bo and Mike have been so helpful and understanding in dealing with our booking. We arrived from Albany with a confirmed 7 night booking for our visit to see my sick mother. When she passed we had to extend our stay and went to the office to let them know. However they had already tentatively booked our extra time so we would not have to move on. They were also able to assist in booking a chalet for other family who were joining us for the funeral. Outstanding caring service. Thank you.
Lady in office needs customer service advice not very friendly
The size of powered sites is decent. However, the facility is quite aged and not well maintained and cleaned. It is a bit below the average quality of Big4 parks.

Spent five nights here in our camper.  It is a busy place with a great beachside location some 10-15 minutes north of the city.  The sites are of a good size so it is not too crowded even when busy.  It has excellent amenities with very good hot water.

Called to book on the phone.
Have just moved to town and needed a place to camp for a few days, the woman on reception interrogated me as to why i wanted to come stay at the park.
Very rude and totally inappropriate.

Unhelpful in person too, other park stayers said the same thing.
Not that hard to be nice to paying customers.

We really enjoyed our relaxing stay at Sunset Beach Holiday Park. The booking and check in were a breeze and our cabin had everything we required. It was so quiet and peaceful with lovely beds-best sleep ever! It was an easy walk into town where we enjoyed the Shoreleave festival, interesting museum, ANZAC day parade, art exhibitions and great food. Highly recommended.

Shout out to Sunset Caravan park, your understanding on our recent stay & being able to extend our stay at such little notice, especially through school holidays, was so very much appreciated & the compassion your staff showed was truly lovely & much appreciated.  We had a lovely shady spot on a big sites which was awesome . Your amazing cleaners are so good  & such lovely ladies too!! Thank you so much we loved our stay . Geraldton is so underrated it’s a beautiful spot & so glad we didn’t listen to other people telling us to bypass Geraldton

Amazing spot. Unfortunately I did not have much time to enjoy it. I recommend not only because of the location, but also for the infrastructure, options of accommodation there. I'll come back for sure!
One of our favourite stops. They have clearly put effort into this place - great playground and pool set next to an awesome beach. We will be back!

Site could use some TLC as it was not very even, grassed or well presented.
Amenities whilst older had good flowing hot water and were clean and presentable.
Enjoyed our stay. There are a couple of well equipped camp kitchens and BBQ areas. Amenities are aged but work well and are clean.   Would be happy to stay again
Just out of town, nice & quiet. In need of a bit of an upgrade, but very functional and good value.

Grounds men and cleaners are very welcoming and happy, the costumer service was lacking a lot. Asked for a room upgrade for 8 days was told fully booked but walking around every night and no one in the larger cabins. Every time went to the office there was always questions asked about why we needed something (felt like some things were a bit too personal).
The smaller room we were put in was falling apart on the deck the cabin leans to one side, very cramped with a double bed, bunk bed and a couch, kitchen is very small as well. Not really designed for a family (more for a single person).
The cabins along the front also has a lot of traffic passing by (not recommended for young children)

Hatten schönes, gut eingerichtetes Motel Studio. Bequemes Bett und alles hat funktioniert. Sehr freundlicher, unkomplizierter Check-In.
Einzig hat der Security Nachtwächter gestört, der sich nachts mit Film anschauen wach hielt (was wir grundsätzlich verstehen, nur war es zu laut, zu lange und zu nahe).
Der Aufenthalt hat uns dennoch sehr gefallen.

Very nice, clean,well maintained park.
Pet friendly, child friendly.
Located on the beach with, I'm told, good fishing from the beach. Fish cleaning area close to camp kitchen. You can opt for a van site with an ensuite
Try staying a few days
I think you'll like it.

Un bel campeggio, abbiamo trovato tutto pulito ed in ordine. Il campeggio domenica 27 gennaio era quasi vuoto dopo il rientro degli Australiani. Manca una stella perché stanno facendo lavori alla spiaggia.

Plenty of space between plots for caravans they have individual and block of chalets for long or short stays. Right on the beach. Great for viewing sunsets. Sandy beach means not so clear water onshore as in town where the reefs keep the sand towards the beach. You can’t have everything 20 min walk to I G A supermarket.

Had an overnighter in the first cabin on the right as you enter the park on trip heading north. Ocean view from the deck and a short walk to have a bit of a fishing session. The cabin is self contained, air conditioned and has comphy bed plus bunks if you need them. Was a really nice stay, staff were friendly.

Stayed with my family in this BIG4 Sunset Beach Holiday Park Geraldton for 4 nights in a deluxe accommodation during last Christmas break. We enjoyed a lot. Paula the receptionist is an amazing lady, very polite and the customer service we received from her was amazing. Rooms are nice and cosy. I will never hesitate to recommend this BIG4 to my friends and family's. WELL DONE Paula.
Kind regards,
Ali Mir.
Also a reliable place to stay. Great rooms and location. So close to the beach you can hear the waves and smell the salty air!
We simply love it here. Fantastic amenities, happy staff, perfect location. Thank you for the upgrades to the playground.

Rather rude service, having spent nearly 5 minutes on hold after enquiring for a site, then same person picks up and starts whole conversation again in a rather exasperated manner. Similarly dour faced in person.
Site is ok, feels very run down and neglected in parts, shower was very slow and ages to heat up.
Well located on the beach, however there's heavy erosion and it similarly looks a bit tired, but I can't blame that on the campsite :-)
Overall not the worst site we've used in WA, but equally not the best.
Always love staying here, great place, good amenities, right on the beach and a short drive into the town  centre.
So far so good
Stayed in campervan at this location, great spot near beach, good facilities only downside was no cooling in camp kitchen so was boiling in there.
Very spacious chalets, lovely layout, clean and tidy close to the 🏖
A lovely dog friendly caravan park right on the water.

Spent a night here on serviced caravan site. Very disappointed to hear from other guests that there had been a lot of theft in the park the night before we arrived...car broken in to on the site we were on! Asked manager why we weren't at least warned to lock up our stuff at night. A basic duty of care!!! Manager was indifferent....I quote... "It happens everywhere. Cockroaches come out at night"...not helpful guys. Not recommended.
close to the beach

Great caravan park, playground was fantastic for the kids and the ensuite powered site for our caravan was perfect for us.  Ablution blocks and ensuites are pretty dated but all clean and tidy. Beautiful sunsets and lookout
Walk to beach. Iga and fuel station within minutes.
Fantastic loved it here. Wish we were here longer. Love that it is dog friendly too.
Good old but very clean amenities sites are generous and have BBQs pool great kitchen a sunset deck and a pool table

A nice 2 night stay at Sunset Beach Holiday Park.
My room was clean and comfortable, with all amenities available.
Accommodation is a short walk to the beach, which I enjoyed walking along.
The rooms are good. It's on the beach and the view is Bueno ♥️

Very overpriced for a tiny bedsit. I normally stay at another park where I get a 2 bedroom chalet for a lot less money. The TV remote didn't work, the office said they would be over when they closed, nothing. Very disappointed.

Great location, right by the  beach. Not far from a supermarket and a close drive to town. The cabin was clean, and had its own kitchen & bathroom. The beds were comfortable. The shower had great pressure! The cabin we stayed in was old, but catered to our needs

Een camping die een overnachting waard is. De locatie is top precies naast het strand. Ook de kampkeuken is uitstekend. Sanitair is prima. De ontvangst was een stuk minder, de receptioniste had niet veel zin om te werken en keek ons niet eens aan. "Oei, 1 nacht overnachten met een campervan? Moeilijk moeilijk er is niet veel plaats..."
Blijkt het camperveld zo goed als leeg te zijn! Beetje duur betaald voor wat je krijgt.

What an uninviting, cranky Receptionist this place has.
I felt like a Pariah when I arrived 2/8/23 , or was it because she just she hated Overnighters.
Must have been the latter, she gave me a spot (110) directly opposite the toilets.
The tanks needed emptying, which was very unpleasant.
Never again.
Excellent caravan park with access to the beach. Good sized space for vans. Reasonable tariff $42 for a powered site. Good amenities. Dog friendly.

3 night stay
Uneven sites couldn't open door on concrete slab as van too long, despite telling lady at reception it was 26ft including A frame. slab cracked .
Toilets floors wet and slippery had been washed but no attempt to dry them.
Thought Big 4 better than that
Very disappointed
Would not recommend for the price
Very convenient to get to and very easy site access. Ablutions nice and clean.
Good to hear the waves on the beach at night

Good park at the beach. Sites are mostly sand/gravel
Clean facilities
It is a bit cramped
Nice, clean. Good little camp

It's a bit dated but clean. The overall impression is a bit sad. Around the amenieties a constant creaking from the ventilation on top of the roof of the amenities can be heard - rather unnerving like an alarm clock goung berserk, but in irregular rhythm.
Shower doors don't close, a lock almost came off while I tried to close it.
The camp kitchen was dark in the morning of a cloudy and rainy day because lights come off automatically. The murky atmosphere was not very inviting to make and have breakfast there. Also you would have had a hard time seeing what you were cooking.
I usually prefer to give feedback directly in the reception when checking out. The lady there however was totally uninterested. For the substandard offered it's clearly overpriced.

I've booked and stayed here for the last 4 years and every time they've changed the motel room I booked. I require a room I can park my 4WD and boat outside for security as it's loaded on my trip north and on the return trip. It's always an issue even though I nominate a particular room and book it 9 months prior. There is allegedly a crime and stealing problem in the area hence my caution. The rooms are looking very tired and run down and not good value for $175 a night. There's better around Geraldton.

Perfect location right On the beach. Great playground for the kids.
Great place to stay. Maybe just needed extra blankets for warm during the colder months!
